description: Pairwise ingredient mixing rules. Each ingredient belongs to one or more groups; rules are defined between groups. Severity: avoid | caution | great | essential.
disclaimer: Educational reference only, not medical advice. Tolerance is individual. When in doubt, patch test and introduce one active at a time.

rules.reason: Stacking a retinoid with AHAs in the same routine can cause cumulative irritation and over-exfoliation.
rules.recommendation: Alternate on different nights (e.g. acids Mon/Wed/Fri, retinoid Tue/Thu/Sat). Buffer with moisturizer.

rules.reason: Both increase cell turnover and can together overwhelm the barrier, especially when starting out.
rules.recommendation: Use on alternate nights, or BHA in the AM and retinoid in the PM if tolerated.

rules.reason: Pure L-ascorbic acid works best at low pH while retinoids prefer a higher pH; layered together they can irritate.
rules.recommendation: Split them: vitamin C in the morning, retinoid at night.

rules.reason: Benzoyl peroxide can oxidize and deactivate older retinoids like retinol, and the combo is drying. (Note: adapalene is specifically stable with BPO.)
rules.recommendation: Use BPO in the AM and retinol in the PM. Adapalene + BPO together is fine and well studied.

rules.reason: Benzoyl peroxide oxidizes ascorbic acid, neutralizing both ingredients.
rules.recommendation: Use at completely separate times (different parts of the day) or on different days.

rules.reason: Both are acidic; layering raises the total acid load and the chance of stinging or irritation.
rules.recommendation: Use vitamin C in the AM and acids in the PM, or alternate days.

rules.reason: The classic 'don't mix' warning is a debunked myth; modern stable formulas use both together for brightening.
rules.recommendation: Safe to layer. If you notice flushing with a homemade mix, simply space them a few minutes apart.

rules.reason: Copper peptides and ascorbic acid can form inactive complexes, reducing the benefit of both.
rules.recommendation: Use copper peptides at night and vitamin C in the morning.

rules.reason: The low pH of direct acids can disrupt the copper-peptide bond.
rules.recommendation: Separate into different routines (AM/PM) for best stability.

rules.reason: Both are potent; using together adds little and may irritate sensitive skin.
rules.recommendation: Alternate routines — peptides one night, retinoid the next.

rules.reason: Salicylic acid clears pores while niacinamide calms inflammation and regulates oil — an ideal acne duo.
rules.recommendation: Layer freely; great for oily and acne-prone skin.

rules.reason: Niacinamide pairs with hyaluronic acid and other humectants for barrier support and hydration.
rules.recommendation: Layer humectant first on damp skin, then niacinamide.

rules.reason: Humectants and ceramides buffer retinoid dryness and reduce irritation.
rules.recommendation: Apply a hydrating layer before or after the retinoid (the 'sandwich' method).

rules.reason: Ceramides, cholesterol and squalane restore the barrier that retinoids can temporarily compromise.
rules.recommendation: Follow your retinoid with a ceramide moisturizer.

rules.reason: Peptides support collagen alongside a retinoid without competing chemically.
rules.recommendation: Layer peptides under the retinoid, or use peptides AM and retinoid PM.

rules.reason: Centella, allantoin and oat calm the redness and irritation a retinoid can cause.
rules.recommendation: Layer a soothing serum before the retinoid to improve tolerance.

rules.reason: AHAs thin the surface layer and increase UV sensitivity for up to a week.
rules.recommendation: Daily broad-spectrum SPF is non-negotiable when using acids.

rules.reason: Retinoids increase photosensitivity and are degraded by UV.
rules.recommendation: Use retinoids at night and wear SPF every morning.

rules.reason: Both calm redness and target acne and pigmentation gently — a strong sensitive-skin combo.
rules.recommendation: Layer freely, AM or PM.

rules.reason: Azelaic acid is gentle and complements retinoids for acne and tone without harsh exfoliation.
rules.recommendation: Can be layered, or azelaic AM and retinoid PM if sensitive.

rules.reason: Stable vitamin C derivatives layer comfortably with niacinamide for brightening.
rules.recommendation: Layer freely in the AM under sunscreen.
